organism: Neisseria meningitidis serogroup B (strain ATCC BAA-335 / MC58)

machinery_genes.role: inner-membrane ABC ATPase that energises export and forms the IM channel
machinery_genes.quote: An ORF encoding a predicted protein of 742 amino acids (aa) that was 70% identical to E. coli HlyB over 678 aa was detected in both genomes
machinery_genes.note: Locus tag NMB1400 in the MC58 (group B) genome, the exact strain of this instance. Wooldridge et al 2005 identify it as the HlyB (ATP-binding) component of the meningococcal T1SS; mutation abolishes FrpC secretion.

machinery_genes.role: periplasm-spanning, IM-anchored adaptor bridging the ABC transporter to the OMP
machinery_genes.quote: A gene with similarity to the E. coli hlyD gene was also found in both genomes ... the equivalent gene in strain MC58 was intact and predicted to encode a protein of 475 aa with 39% identity to E. coli HlyD over 468 aa
machinery_genes.note: Locus tag NMB1738 in MC58. Membrane fusion protein component of the meningococcal T1SS; mutation at this locus abolishes FrpC secretion.

machinery_genes.role: TolC-family outer-membrane channel forming the exit duct
machinery_genes.quote: The hlyD homolog was adjacent to a homolog of tolC, which is unlinked to the hemolysin secretion genes of E. coli.
machinery_genes.note: Locus tag NMB1737 in MC58 (immediately downstream of hlyD), identified as the closest tolC match (27.6% identity; P = 2.0 x 10-31). Assigned by Wooldridge et al 2005 specifically to this meningococcal T1SS (not a shared efflux annotation); mutation abolishes FrpC secretion.

notes: This instance is the Neisseria meningitidis serogroup B MC58 type I secretion system that exports the iron-repressible RTX exoprotein FrpC (locus NMB1415, the curated effector). The apparatus paper (Wooldridge et al 2005, Infect Immun) directly searched the MC58 (group B) genome, the exact strain of this instance, so no cross-strain transfer is needed. A T1SS has exactly three core machinery components and all three are documented gene-by-gene with verbatim naming quotes: the HlyB ABC transporter (NMB1400), the HlyD membrane fusion protein (NMB1738), and the TolC outer-membrane channel (NMB1737). The paper shows the genes are unusually scattered (hlyD-tolC adjacent but unlinked to hlyB, and unlinked to the substrate operon) but together encode a functional TOSS required for FrpC/FrpC2 secretion; mutation at either locus abolishes secretion. Deliberately excluded: FrpC itself (NMB1415, the secreted RTX substrate/effector); FrpC2; FrpA (a paralogous RTX substrate, Thompson & Sparling 1993); FrpD (NMB1416, an rtx-locus accessory outer-membrane lipoprotein that binds the FrpC substrate amino-terminus, Prochazkova et al 2004) - FrpD is an accessory substrate-anchoring lipoprotein, not part of the IM/OM export apparatus, so it is not counted as machinery. The 1993 Thompson & Sparling paper is cited as supporting context (it first showed FrpA is secreted by an HlyBD-type system and detected hlyBD-like sequences in meningococci) but the gene-naming evidence for this instance comes from Wooldridge 2005. All quotes verbatim from the cited PMC full text / abstract.
